name: Global Carbon Tax
description: Coordinated carbon pricing at $150/tonne CO₂. Short-term growth drag as energy costs rise; long-term benefit from green investment and efficiency.

name: Global Free Trade Expansion
description: Tariffs fall to zero across 120+ countries. Net global GDP +3–5% long-run, but ~30 countries lose in absolute terms — labour exporters win most, fossil exporters hit hardest.

name: EU Federal Integration
description: EU member states pool debt, defence, and fiscal policy under a shared bond market. Aggregate EU GDP rises 5–8% long-run — but gains are unequal. Southern and eastern members gain most from spread compression and defence pooling. Northern contributors face fiscal transfer drag; Ireland loses most from corporate tax harmonisation. Outside the EU, Russia, Gulf states, and the UK face collateral damage.
